sections.heading: Database User Authentication
sections.body:
Atlas requires clients to authenticate to access
clusters. You must create database users to access the
database. To set up database users for your clusters,
see [Configure Database Users](mongodb-users).

sections.heading: Custom Database Roles
sections.body:
When the built-in Atlas
[built-in role](manual/reference/built-in-roles) don't
meet your desired set of privileges, you can create [custom roles](mongodb-roles).

sections.heading: Authentication with AWS IAM
sections.body:
You can authenticate applications running on AWS (Amazon Web Services) services to Atlas
clusters with AWS (Amazon Web Services) IAM (Identity and Access Management) roles. You can set up a database user to
use an AWS (Amazon Web Services) IAM (Identity and Access Management) role ARN for authentication and connect to your
database using [mongosh](mongodb-shell) and drivers that authenticate using your AWS (Amazon Web Services)
IAM (Identity and Access Management) role ARN. Using AWS (Amazon Web Services) IAM (Identity and Access Management) role reduces the number of
authentication mechanisms and number of secrets to manage.

To learn more, see [AWS IAM Authentication](set-up-pwdless-auth).

sections.heading: User Authentication with LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol)
sections.body:
Atlas supports performing user authentication with LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol). To use LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol),
see Set Up User Authentication and Authorization with LDAP.

sections.heading: User Authentication with OIDC (OpenID Connect)
sections.body:
Atlas supports performing user authentication
with OIDC (OpenID Connect). To use OIDC (OpenID Connect), see [OIDC](oidc-authentication-authorization).

sections.heading: User Authentication with X.509
sections.body:
X.509 client certificates provide database users access to the
clusters in your project. Options for X.509
authentication include Atlas-managed X.509 authentication and
self-managed X.509 authentication. To learn more
about self-managed X.509 authentication, see [self-managed X.509 certificate](self-managed-x509).
